title: trachylomia triglomerata
intro: Trachylomia triglomerata, commonly known as the Spiny Beggartick, is a plant species in the dandelion family that is native to China. This plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its health benefits, which include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as promoting digestion and relieving rheumatism. The therapeutic actions of Trachylomia triglomerata include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and calm various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and health benefits.

side_effects: Trachylomia triglomerata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high toxicity levels. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's leaves or flowers can cause severe abdominal pain, headaches, and dizziness.
